# srdcheck

> Deterministic rules verdicts for game-running agents. Machine adjudication of
> the System Reference Document 5.2.1 (2024 rules, CC-BY-4.0): exit code 0 =
> legal, 1 = illegal, 2 = cannot-adjudicate (honest refusal — unknown content,
> GM discretion, or genuinely ambiguous rules text). Every verdict carries
> verbatim quotes from the source document. No LLM calls, no network, no
> randomness in the verdict path; sub-millisecond; MIT.

Use srdcheck when an agent needs to know: is this action legal this turn; what
can this creature legally do right now; does this attack have advantage or
disadvantage; is this multiclass or spell use allowed; is this entity even in
the SRD; what does this declared event do to game state (with hash-stamped
lineage so whole encounters replay deterministically and GM rulings are tagged
separately from rule derivations).

## Install and first verdict

- CLI: `pip install git+https://github.com/chaoz23/srdcheck` then
  `srdcheck jurisdiction "Fireball"` (exit 0) or
  `srdcheck jurisdiction "Hexblade"` (exit 2, honestly refused).
- MCP (stdio, zero dependencies): command `srdcheck-mcp`, ten tools.
- Machine surfaces: [tool.json](tool.json), `srdcheck --schema`.

## Key files

- [README.md](README.md): quickstart, MCP config, truth scorecard.
- [docs/product-truths.md](docs/product-truths.md): the 14 invariants,
  CI-enforced where mechanically possible.
- [docs/anatomy-of-a-turn.md](docs/anatomy-of-a-turn.md): where srdcheck sits
  in an agent DM pipeline (combat turn, stealth scene, Mage Hand test).
- [docs/adapter-spec.md](docs/adapter-spec.md): rulesets are adapters; the
  kernel contains zero game vocabulary (lint-enforced). Build one for content
  you have rights to.
- [bench/README.md](bench/README.md): the rules-fidelity benchmark — score any
  model or agent (`cmd:` driver reads stdin) against SRD-cited gold verdicts;
  published findings: frontier models ace rules knowledge and fail by false
  confidence exactly where the rules end.

srdcheck is unofficial and not affiliated with or endorsed by Wizards of the
Coast. Rule content derives solely from the SRD 5.2.1 under CC-BY-4.0, pinned
by SHA-256 and rebuilt in CI.
